struct FenwickTree {
    size: usize,
    tree: Vec<i64>,
}

impl FenwickTree {
    fn new(size: usize) -> Self {
        let mut tree = vec![0; size + 1];
        Self { size, tree }
    }

    fn update(&mut self, index: usize, delta: i64) {
        let i = index + 1; // 1-based index
        while i <= self.size {
            self.tree[i] += delta;
            i += i & -i;
        }
    }

    fn query(&self, index: usize) -> i64 {
        let mut sum = 0;
        let i = index + 1;
        while i > 0 {
            sum += self.tree[i];
            i -= i & -i;
        }
        sum
    }

    fn range_query(&self, l: usize, r: usize) -> i64 {
        if l > r {
            return 0;
        }
        self.query(r) - self.query(l - 1)
    }
}

pub fn solve(nums: Vec<i64>, queries: Vec<Vec<i64>>) -> Vec<i64> {
    let n = nums.len();
    let mut depths = vec![0; n];
    for i in 0..n {
        depths[i] = compute_depth(nums[i]);
    }

    let mut bits: Vec<FenwickTree> = vec![FenwickTree::new(n); 6];
    for i in 0..n {
        let d = depths[i];
        if d <= 5 {
            bits[d as usize].update(i, 1);
        }
    }

    let mut answer = Vec::new();

    for q in queries {
        if q[0] == 1 {
            let l = q[1] as usize;
            let r = q[2] as usize;
            let k = q[3] as usize;
            let res = bits[k].range_query(l, r);
            answer.push(res);
        } else {
            let idx = q[1] as usize;
            let val = q[2];
            let old_d = depths[idx];
            if old_d <= 5 {
                bits[old_d as usize].update(idx, -1);
            }
            let new_d = compute_depth(val);
            depths[idx] = new_d;
            if new_d <=5 {
                bits[new_d as usize].update(idx, 1);
            }
        }
    }

    answer
}

struct FenwickTree {
    n: usize,
    tree: Vec<i64>,
}

impl FenwickTree {
    fn new(size: usize) -> Self {
        let mut tree = vec![0; size + 1];
        FenwickTree { n: size, tree }
    }

    fn update(&mut self, idx: usize, delta: i64) {
        let idx = idx + 1; // 1-based index
        while idx <= self.n {
            self.tree[idx] += delta;
            idx += idx & -idx;
        }
    }

    fn query(&self, idx: usize) -> i64 {
        let idx = idx + 1; // 1-based index
        let mut res = 0;
        while idx > 0 {
            res += self.tree[idx];
            idx -= idx & -idx;
        }
        res
    }

    fn range_query(&self, l: usize, r: usize) -> i64 {
        self.query(r) - self.query(l - 1)
    }
}

pub fn solve(nums: Vec<i64>, queries: Vec<Vec<i64>>) -> Vec<i64> {
    let n = nums.len();
    let mut fenwick_trees: Vec<FenwickTree> = vec![FenwickTree::new(n); 6];

    for i in 0..n {
        let x = nums[i];
        let depth = compute_depth(x);
        if depth >= 0 && depth <= 5 {
            let k = depth as usize;
            fenwick_trees[k].update(i, 1);
        }
    }

    let mut answer = Vec::new();

    for query in queries {
        if query[0] == 1 {
            let l = query[1] as usize;
            let r = query[2] as usize;
            let k = query[3] as usize;
            let res = fenwick_trees[k].range_query(l, r);
            answer.push(res);
        } else {
            let idx = query[1] as usize;
            let val = query[2];
            let old_depth = compute_depth(nums[idx]);
            if old_depth >= 0 && old_depth <= 5 {
                let k_old = old_depth as usize;
                fenwick_trees[k_old].update(idx, -1);
            }
            let new_depth = compute_depth(val);
            if new_depth >= 0 && new_depth <= 5 {
                let k_new = new_depth as usize;
                fenwick_trees[k_new].update(idx, 1);
            }
            nums[idx] = val;
        }
    }

    answer
}
